merged_events.event_description: George Wadsworth appointed as Agent and Consul General, establishing a quasi-diplomatic liaison presence in Damascus.

evidence.quote: The United States appointed George Wadsworth as "Agent and Consul General" on October 9, 1942, to provide a quasi-diplomatic presence in Damascus until the United States determined that Syria achieved effective independence in 1944.

merged_events.event_description: The United States established diplomatic relations with Syria and opened the American Legation when George Wadsworth presented his credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: The United States established diplomatic relations with Syria when George Wadsworth presented his credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary on November 17, 1944.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations with Syria ended and the American Embassy in Damascus was reclassified as a Consulate General after Syria joined Egypt to form the United Arab Republic.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations with Syria ended and the American Embassy in Damascus was reclassified as a Consulate General on February 25, 1958, after Syria joined Egypt to form the new state, the United Arab Republic.

merged_events.event_description: The United States reestablished diplomatic relations with the Syrian Arab Republic and the American Consulate General at Damascus was raised to Embassy status, with Ridgway B. Knight as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: The United States reestablished diplomatic relations with the Syrian Arab Republic and the American Consulate General at Damascus was raised to Embassy Status on October 10, 1961, with Ridgway B. Knight as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: Syria severed diplomatic relations with the United States in the wake of the June 1967 Arab-Israeli War, ending all formal representation.

evidence.quote: Syria severed diplomatic relations with the United States on June 6, 1967 in the wake of the June 1967 Arab-Israeli War.

merged_events.event_description: A U.S. Interests Section in Syria was established in the Italian Embassy with Thomas J. Scotes as Principal Officer.

evidence.quote: A U.S. Interests Section in Syria was established on February 8, 1974, in the Italian Embassy with Thomas J. Scotes as Principal Officer.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations and the American Embassy in Damascus were reestablished with Scotes as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations and the American Embassy in Damascus were reestablished on June 16, 1974, with Scotes as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: George Wadsworth presented credentials as Diplomatic Agent/Consul General, establishing a liaison-level U.S. presence in Syria.

evidence.quote: George Wadsworth, Diplomatic Agent/Consul General. Appointed: 1942-10-09. Credentials presented: 1942-11-30.

merged_events.event_description: James Sayle Moose was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented credentials, elevating the U.S. mission from a legation to an embassy.

evidence.quote: James Sayle Moose, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1952-06-25. Credentials presented: 1952-08-14. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1952-09-30.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. Embassy in Damascus suspended operations and all U.S. personnel were withdrawn due to deteriorating security conditions during the Syrian civil war.

evidence.quote: [Mission Note] The U.S. Embassy in Damascus suspended operations on February 6, 2012, and all U.S. personnel were withdrawn after security conditions deteriorated in the midst of the Syrian civil war.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1944-11-17) and status (Legation). Wadsworth presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary.
Exact match on date (1958-02-25). CSV says 'Consulate'; RDCR says 'Consulate General' — consistent with CSV status category conventions.
Exact match on date (1961-10-10) and status (Embassy). Note: Event 3 has a citation error flag, but the described content is fully consistent with the CSV event.
Exact match on date (1967-06-06) and status (None). Syria severed diplomatic relations.
Exact match on date (1974-02-08) and status (Interests). U.S. Interests Section established in Italian Embassy.
Exact match on date (1974-06-16) and status (Embassy). Diplomatic relations and Embassy reestablished.
Exact match on date (2012-02-06) and status (None). Embassy suspended operations; sourced from POCOM mission note.

missing_from_csv.event_description: George Wadsworth presented credentials as Diplomatic Agent/Consul General, establishing a quasi-diplomatic liaison presence in Damascus prior to formal recognition of Syrian independence. RDCR gives appointment date 1942-10-09; POCOM gives credential presentation date 1942-11-30.
missing_from_csv.notes: RDCR's date (1942-10-09) is the appointment date, which is a personnel event, not an institutional opening date. For initial establishment of a new presence, the credential presentation date from POCOM (1942-11-30) is the more appropriate institutional date. Strong candidate for addition to CSV as a Liaison-level event preceding the 1944 Legation.

missing_from_csv.event_description: James Sayle Moose was promoted from Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, indicating elevation of the U.S. mission from Legation to Embassy status.
missing_from_csv.notes: Only found in POCOM; RDCR does not mention this elevation. The date (1952-09-30) is a promotion date (personnel event), not an explicit institutional transition date. However, the CSV currently jumps from Legation (1944) to Consulate (1958) with no intervening Embassy status, which is historically implausible — the legation was almost certainly elevated to an embassy before being reclassified as a consulate in 1958. Candidate for addition, though the exact institutional date may need further research.
